metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated operational pain around manual supplier onboarding, contract management inefficiency, and lack of centralized intake, but never quantified the impact in cost, headcount savings, cycle-time reduction, or ROI. Christina stated on the 17 Dec 2025 discovery call that Pep needs iSupplier for supplier onboarding, iContract for contract lifecycle management, and Intake for request centralization (Next Step: 'strong understand of need'). The 16 Jan 2026 demo refined scope to 'iSupplier/iRisk lite/iContract' (Next Step), confirming pain areas. However, no buyer has stated a dollar value, error rate, or savings target across all calls and Next Step entries. Grace noted 28 Jan 2026 'prioritizing timeline conversation ahead of demo session to align on priorities' (Next Step), signaling an attempt to quantify value, but no follow-up evidence of quantified metrics appears. Without a quantified business case, budget approval and EB sign-off remain blocked. The absence of metrics is a knowledge gap (not merely a hygiene gap), as no Salesforce field (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c) or call transcript contains numerical impact.

champion.narrative: Christina Behm (Sourcing Operations Manager, Decision Maker per Contact Role) has acted as the primary champion and drove all buyer-side activity from Nov 2025 through May 2026, but she is now cooling and showing signs of losing internal sponsorship or deprioritization. Christina initiated the NDA (8 Dec 2025, Next Step), attended discovery (17 Dec) and both demos (16 Jan), requested pricing (13 Feb), and maintained regular cadence through May 2026. She stated 26 May 2026 'working through internal approvals, will reach out with update shortly' (Next Step), signaling active advocacy, but then went silent for 24 days until 9 Jun 2026 ('will connect end of month', Next Step), and has not responded to Grace's 29 Jun outreach (8-day non-response as of 7 Jul). Christina has no documented access to the Economic Buyer (no CFO/CPO engagement), and her ability to drive the decision is constrained by the opaque 'internal approvals' process she cited. She has not introduced Zycus to senior execs, shared internal docs, or pushed back on competitors (the internal build option remains live per 17 Dec Tim statement, Next Step). VP Tim Drost, who could have been an executive sponsor, disengaged after 17 Dec and did not respond to 30 Apr re-engagement attempts (Next Step). Christina's current engagement is passive at best, and the lack of EB access, combined with her recent silence and delayed reconnect promise ('3rd week of July', post-close date), signals she may be losing internal traction or the deal is deprioritized. Champion strength is weak and at risk.

competition.narrative: The primary competitor is an internal build by the relevant stakeholder team extending their existing 'Pep Connect' system, which remains a live, unresolved option and poses an existential risk to the deal. VP Tim Drost stated on the 17 Dec 2025 discovery call, 'we looked at building this, and it still could happen' (Next Step), confirming the build-vs-buy decision was not closed at that time. No subsequent Next Step entry or Salesforce field (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c) updates the build-vs-buy status, meaning the internal build remains a live threat 6.5+ months later. Tim also stated 17 Dec 'evaluating multiple vendors' (Next Step), but no other external vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer) are named in any Next Step, task, or Salesforce field, so the primary competition is the internal build, not an external rival. Zycus has not addressed the build threat directly: no ROI case comparing Zycus TCO vs build cost, no time-to-value vs build timeline, no risk-of-build narrative (maintenance, scalability, AI/Merlin differentiation) appears in any Next Step or open deliverable. The wedge to win is to position Zycus as the faster, lower-risk path with AI/Merlin capabilities Pep cannot build, but this narrative has not been activated. Without resolving the build-vs-buy decision and securing exec buy-in, the deal could vanish into an internal project with no warning.

hard.ais_why:
• Across the 89-minute 2/13/2026 demo, Pep stakeholders Tim Drost, Jillian Lushman, and Christina Behm did not voice a single AI, Merlin, Agentic, ANA, Intake, or GenAI reference. The full conversation revolved around iSupplier, iContract, and iRisk workflows: onboarding, due diligence, contract review, and supplier risk integrations with EcoVadis and DNB.
• Buyer questions were process-mechanical: white-labelling, conditional questioning, delegation and impersonation, predetermined categories, and IT questionnaire integrations. Tim's framing question was "How do you guys bundle this thing up to sell it?" focused on commercial packaging. Even with 89 minutes on the clock, no one asked about agents or Merlin Intake.
• Confirmed next steps lock the direction: selection of iContract, iSupplier, and iRisk modules, then literature and a quote. That matches the original scope of iSupplier, iRisk Lite, and iContract. No POC, no AI line items, and no champion advocating for AI capability.
